title: Add reference to FDE Interview Handbook for Forward Deployed Engineer roles
author: cma0232
body:
## Summary

FDE (Forward Deployed Engineer) interviews cover a unique set of topics not addressed in most SWE guides — agentic system design, live demo rounds, customer-facing case studies, and LLM deployment questions. This is a growing role category at companies like Palantir, Databricks, Scale AI, Anduril, and Google.

I'd like to add a short section to the README pointing readers toward the [FDE Interview Handbook](https://github.com/cma0232/fde-interview-prep) — a free, curated resource specifically for FDE interview prep.

This is especially useful for software engineers pivoting into Forward Deployed Engineer roles — where your code ships inside customer environments, not just your own infrastructure.

**Proposed addition:** A brief "Looking for Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) content?" section, similar to the existing "Looking for Front End content?" section.

Happy to submit a PR if this is welcome!

title: Suggestion: Add StreamPrep as a structured learning path resource
author: usubhash
body:
Hi! Thanks for building and maintaining this handbook — it’s one of the best interview prep resources.

I’d like to suggest adding [StreamPrep](https://streamprep.dev) as a complementary resource for users who want a structured roadmap (week-by-week flow across coding, system design, and behavioral prep).

Why it could fit this repo:
- This repo is excellent breadth/reference
- StreamPrep can help users who ask “what should I do each day/week?”
- It complements Grind/handbook-style prep with guided progression

If helpful, I can open a tiny PR with a single bullet in the relevant resources section.

Suggested text:
`- [StreamPrep](https://streamprep.dev) - Structured interview prep roadmap with guided progression across coding, system design, and behavioral practice.`

title: content: fix resume font size recommendation from 10 px to 10 pt
author: eduardo-castro-quispe
body:
# Description

In the resume chapter of the website, specifically under the section ["Only use standard fonts of readable sizes"](https://www.techinterviewhandbook.org/resume/#only-use-standard-fonts-of-readable-sizes), **the guide currently recommends a "minimum font size of 10px" while advising users to create their documents in Microsoft Word or Google Docs.** 

# Issue 

**Standard word processors (Microsoft Word, Google Docs) measure font size in points (pt)**, not pixels (px). Using pixels here is misleading because 10 px converts to approximately 7.5 pt, which is significantly smaller than the standard readable minimum for resumes. Standard advice, such as that from the [MIT CAPD Resume Checklist](https://capd.mit.edu/resources/resume-checklist/), suggests a minimum of 10 pt.

# Suggested Fix

**Update the text to recommend a "minimum of 10 pt" instead of 10 px.**

title: Feature Request: Mark Questions for Review
author: 0p3r4t0r
body:
This feature would be intended to address scenarios like the following:

- You have solved the question, but your solution was not optimal and you want to come back later.
- Although you have solved the question this time, you are not 100% confident you could come up with the solution every time.


Currently, we may mark problems as complete  (green) or incomplete (white). How about adding a third state called review (yellow), to mark problems that you can solve, but still are not 100% confident in?


I would be happy to make a PR for this if it's something we're interested in.

title: Outdated information: Powersort is the default sort implementation since Python 3.11
author: zeroby0
body:
context: 
- https://github.com/yangshun/tech-interview-handbook/blob/main/apps/website/contents/algorithms/sorting-searching.md
- https://www.techinterviewhandbook.org/algorithms/sorting-searching/#things-to-look-out-for-during-interviews

> Bonus points if you can name the sort. In Python, it's [Timsort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Timsort).

The default sorting algorithm has changed to powersort since Python 3.11 (2022)
ref:
- https://www.wild-inter.net/posts/powersort-in-python-3.11
- https://www.i-programmer.info/news/216-python/15954-python-now-uses-powersort.html

title: Grind 75 Feature Request for adding questions

title: Resume Upload Fails with 405 Error
author: nmktad
body:
## Problem Summary
When I try to upload my resume as a PDF, the upload consistently fails. The browser console shows a 405 Method Not Allowed error, which isn't allowing me to submit my resume.

## Steps to Reproduce
Go to the resume submission page: https://app.techinterviewhandbook.org/resumes/submit

- Fill in the "Upload Resume" form on the page. 
- Click the "Upload File" or "Browse" button.
- Choose a PDF file from my computer.
- Click the "Submit" or "Upload" button.

## Expected Behavior
My resume file should upload successfully, and I should see a confirmation message or be taken to a success page.

## Actual Behavior
After clicking "Submit," the page briefly tries to process the upload and a loading state occurs and hangs in that state indefinitely. Then, an error appears in the browser console. No file is uploaded, and there's no error message visible on the webpage itself. The console clearly shows a 405 Method Not Allowed error for the upload request.

title: :warning: Clearbit’s Free Logo API (logo.clearbit.com) is Being Discontinued – Update Required
author: denraf
body:
# :rotating_light: Deprecation Notice :rotating_light:

Clearbit's free Logo API (logo.clearbit.com) will be discontinued on **December 1, 2025**, and will stop working on that date.

## What You Need to Know:
We have identified your project as one that uses logo.clearbit.com - you’ll need to migrate to an alternative solution.
We’ve shared detailed information and alternative approaches in [our announcement on the HubSpot Developer Community](https://developers.hubspot.com/changelog/upcoming-sunset-of-clearbits-free-logo-api).

## Recommended Next Steps
1. **Audit your code**: Identify where logo.clearbit.com is used
2. **Consider alternatives**: Review options and recommendations in our official post
3. **Update dependencies**: If this project is part of a larger system, notify relevant maintainers

We appreciate all the developers who have used this service over the years! Check out the [HubSpot Developer Community](https://developers.hubspot.com/changelog/upcoming-sunset-of-clearbits-free-logo-api) for more details.

title: Hi
author: must-int

title: Matrix cheat sheet suggestion
author: Jomkit
body: Real quick, loving the tech interview handguide! I just wanted to make one suggestion to the matrix cheat sheet page which I think would be really beneficial for users, which is adding some tips about how to find row and column index given overall index in a 2D matrix. I was recently working on leetcode #74 "Search a 2D Matrix" and I was pretty stumped until I read the discussions and someone gave a helpful hint of dividing the overall index by the number of columns to get the row index, and doing a modulo operation by the number of columns. Basically like matrix[index/col][index%col]. I think that would be a really helpful tip for others.

title: Grind75 Leetcode Difficulty Error
author: yeab-dev
body:
On leetcode the problem '[Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree](https://leetcode.com/problems/lowest-common-ancestor-of-a-binary-search-tree/)'  is tagged Medium which is not in line with the grind 75's difficulty tagging for that specific problem.

you can find the problem on the 10th place when the problems are grouped by week and the schedule is 8 weeks and 8 hours per week

title: error: space complexity is mentioned as O(logn) instead of O(n) in Anagrams section of String
author: The-East-Wind
body:
# Issue

In the `Anagrams` section of the [Strings cheatsheet](https://www.techinterviewhandbook.org/algorithms/string/) the space complexity of the approach where sorting both the input strings to determine it they are anagrams or not is mentioned as `O(logn)` but the actual space complexity is `O(n)` (since we'll need 2 additional strings of size `n` for storing sorted copy of the 2 input strings).
